event: The Three Archangels with the young Tobias
symbols: Michael in armor with sword — the warrior archangel, commander of heavenly armies
Raphael in gray robes guiding Tobias — the healing angel who accompanied Tobias on his journey
Gabriel with lily — the angel of the Annunciation bearing the symbol of purity
small white fish — the fish from the Tigris River whose organs healed Tobias's father
small dog — the faithful companion mentioned in the Book of Tobit
chalice — possibly medicine or the healing remedy Raphael taught Tobias to prepare
landscape with mountains — the journey through the world under angelic protection
description: A painting depicting four figures standing in a landscape with mountains in the background. On the left, an angel in shining plate armor holds a sword, with large colorful wings. In the center, a taller angel in flowing gray-blue robes extends a hand to guide a smaller, younger figure in blue who walks beside a small white dog and fish. On the right, a third angel in green and golden robes holds a lily stem, with dark wings. All three angels are youthful with curly hair. The landscape is green and expansive under a pale blue sky.
theological_description: The Three Archangels with young Tobias combines the three named archangels of Scripture with the narrative of Tobit. Michael, the warrior (Revelation 12:7; Daniel 10:13), appears in armor as the defender against evil. Raphael, whose name means 'God heals,' is the angel who accompanied Tobias on his journey to Media, protected him from danger, and taught him the remedy that would heal his father Tobit's blindness (Tobit 5-12). Gabriel, the messenger (Luke 1:26), carries the lily of the Annunciation. Together they represent the three primary modes of angelic ministry: protection (Michael), healing and guidance (Raphael), and revelation (Gabriel). The young Tobias walking with Raphael illustrates the theology of angelic guardianship: 'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways' (Psalm 91:11). The dog and fish are specific narrative details from Tobit — the fish from the Tigris whose heart and liver drove away the demon Asmodeus (Tobit 6:5-8), and the dog that accompanied the travelers (Tobit 6:2). Hebrews 1:14 summarizes the angelic role: 'Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?'

scripture_refs.reason: Michael and his angels fighting the dragon — Michael's identity as heavenly warrior
Gabriel sent to announce the Incarnation — Gabriel's role as divine messenger with the lily
He will command his angels to guard you — the theology of angelic guardianship depicted in Raphael guiding Tobias
Michael as chief prince who aids in spiritual warfare — the armored archangel
Angels as ministering spirits sent to serve those who inherit salvation — the unified role of the three archangels

_meta.title: Die Drei Erzengel Mit Dem Kleinen Tobias
